items.title: Ravenswatch Critic Reviews
items.summary: Ravenswatch has been reviewed by 29 critics and currently has a
items.content:
It may not have the same narrative pull as games like Hades (despite having a compelling setup to do so), but Ravenswatch places its focus on combat. Admittedly, the moment-to-moment gameplay and impressively large pool of upgrades and run modifiers is what made Passtech's last roguelike, Curse of the Dead Gods, such a surprise hit among fans of the genre. Ravenswatch only improves on that formula while also continuing Passtech's trademark panache for beautiful cel-shaded visuals and eye-catching animations. It might still need some wrinkles ironed out in terms of its balance and difficulty, but otherwise, Ravenswatch is a strong action roguelike and a worthwhile entry into the increasingly crowded genre.

Ravenswatch is a solid tried and true roguelike with many different ways to play through the options of heroes available to players. It's difficult and rewarding for solo or co-op parties and does its best to be an interesting experience. With the diverse cast of heroes and challenging side quests, it's hard not to queue up several runs a day. Every character we have was given the care of a compelling story on why and how their tale took a darker turn. We see the developer taking something old and making it new for us once again. It's truly awesome to see old stories given new life to become something else altogether.

Ravenswatch is an absolute blast. Gorgeous to look at, smooth and responsive to play, and peppered with the kind of variety that encourages you to keep going back

Ravenswatch is an excellent roguelike, one that's filled with clever gameplay design, lovely art, and just a really cool core concept. Now that it's fully out in the wild, I'm happy to wholeheartedly recommend it to basically anyone who's even tangentially interested, and while I do sort of hope they keep releasing new characters, it already feels more than complete.

Ravenswatch is a lot of fun to play, I unlocked all of the playable characters and played with each and had a good time with all of them. The randomly generated nature of each level is nice but the truth of the matter is this game is very short. And while I recommend that players play multiplayer, the game does offer the option to play single-player but it’s hard as balls. I am sure some of you who love these brutal difficulty games will enjoy, but for those of us who have yet to ascend that difficulty mountain, dis tew much. All that being said though, I like Ravenswatch quite a bit. While my time with it is short, it was still a pretty enjoyable experience.

I review a lot of games and I’ll admit that diving into another roguelike isn’t always immediately appealing. But Ravenswatch does enough clever things with its mechanics, art, character design, and premise that I was intrigued and engaged. Ravenswatch demonstrates again that in the hands of talented developers, there’s still a lot of untapped potential in the roguelike genre.

Ravenswatch combines cool characters and satisfying combat with challenging boss fights to provide a unique roguelite outing. The dark tone and twist on classic characters adds value and replayability; if only it had local co-op!

An accessible Roguelike that might initially seem a bit on the short side, Ravenswatch has more than ample replay value in the number of characters, modifiers, abilities, and items to experiment with. If you enjoyed Hades and crave roguelike action of a similar ilk, then the fairy tale charms of Ravenswatch will scratch that itch.

Ravenswatch blends remarkable gameplay with an incredible sense of style to make one of the most unique Roguelikes you’ll ever play.

Ravenswatch masterfully blends dark-fantasy storytelling with engaging roguelike mechanics. The unique characters, each with distinct abilities and rich backstories, elevate both the narrative and gameplay. While the secondary missions can feel repetitive, the cooperative dynamics and high replay value make it a standout title for fans of the genre.

Overall, Ravenswatch is a decent game for those who enjoy co-operative action-RPGs and aren't afraid of the trials of a roguelike, but it lacks the polish and variety to qualify for top marks.

What happens when you mix Hades, Diablo, and fairy tales? This is Ravenswatch. Like Supergiant's Hades, Ravenswatch will start as an Early Access game, making it a great place to refine its core mechanics and flesh out its fantasy world. With new characters and gameplay updates coming after the launch of Early Access, Passtech Games has enormous potential for the next promising step towards RPG. They're off to a great start already, and watching that world and its gameplay evolve has got me excited to see where Ravenswatch goes next.

The atmosphere, characters and general style are all high points, but Ravenswatch's major issue comes from how repetitious the gameplay loop is and that rarely anything new or cool pops up. The base structure is there to have been a really cool roguelite, but as it's far more strict in its roguelike nature, it's tough to recommend in any large capacity, lacking in too many things to encourage frequent plays.

Ravenswatch is unique, stylish, and smooth, and its art style is irresistible. It is everything that you would want from an Action Rogulike and then some, and I'm sure you will fall in love with it if you give it a fair chance.

items.title: Ravenswatch Patch Notes (Multiplayer in Russia, Bug Fixes) - Version 1.5.01
items.summary: Greetings, Ravens! Today, we’re pushing a patch to fix a few bugs reported on the Songs of Thieves update, as well as addressing server connection issues for players located in Russia. Patch Notes (1.5.01) 🐦 = Inspired by community reports
items.content:
Greetings, Ravens!

Today, we’re pushing a patch to fix a few bugs reported on the Songs of Thieves update, as well as addressing server connection issues for players located in Russia.

Patch Notes (1.5.01)

🐦 = Inspired by community reports and suggestions

Improvements & Fixed bugs

General

  • No Codes in Multiplayer (Russia): Due to access restrictions beyond our control, some players connecting from Russia were no longer able to access Ravenswatch’s multiplayer mode. With this patch, we have restored multiplayer access for affected players.  Now, when you log in to the game, if the game detects that your connection is blocked by a third-party system, you will automatically be redirected to a second hosting server that is currently not affected by these restrictions.

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Altar of Heroes: Fixed an issue where it was not possible to revive a teammate if they disconnected and reconnected during the interaction

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Stingy Jack: Fixed an issue where engaging Stingy Jack by instantly staggering him would not trigger his countdown

    [/*]

Melodies

  • 🐦 Notes: Fixed an issue where Notes could spawn OOB in Dark Hill’s cursed rooms

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Harps: Fixed an issue where Vision Towers would not reveal wandering Harps

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Galahad Melody: Fixed an issue where Galahad Melody would break the Altar of Heroes flow

    [/*]

Heroes

  • 🐦 The Pied Piper: Fixed an issue where Rats would not respawn properly after teleporting from a remote area (caves, thieves' rooms, etc.)

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Snow Queen: Fixed an issue where the ‘Slide’ Talent was not counted for the ‘Moonstone’ Magical Object

    [/*]

  • Sun Wukong:

    • 🐦 Fixed an issue where the Magic Mirror’s UI would remain on the screen after the ‘Transfiguration’ effect ended

      [/*]

    • 🐦 Fixed an issue where DMG and VITALITY earned by the ‘Philosopher’s Stone’ and the ‘Hungry Grass’ Magical Objects were discarded after each ‘Transfiguration’ effect ended

      [/*]
    [/*]

Community Spotlight

In case you missed it, we recently shared a Community Spotlight that takes a look at player statistics from the “Songs of Thieves” update, the Nintendo Switch 2, and more:

  • The Passtech Team 🍉

    [/*]

items.title: Ravenswatch Community Spotlight (June 2026)
items.summary: Greetings, Ravens! It's a new Community Spotlight! We're slowly coming back down to earth since the release of the Songs of Thieves update last month... But we've got a ton of things to cover, so let's not waste a minute! Today's Community
items.content:
Greetings, Ravens!

It's a new Community Spotlight! We're slowly coming back down to earth since the release of the Songs of Thieves update last month... But we've got a ton of things to cover, so let's not waste a minute!

Today's Community Spotlight is about:

  • New episode of News from Reverie

    [/*]

  • Songs of Thieves Statistics

    [/*]

  • Monthly Challenges

    [/*]

  • Community Creations

    [/*]

  • What's next! (Hero DLC & Nintendo Switch 2 Edition)

    [/*]

News from Reverie (June 2026)

A new episode of News from Reverie is available! In this video, Margaux (Communications Director) goes through all the topics of the Community Spotlight and shares more about what's next for Ravenswatch.

Remember to subscribe to our YouTube channel so you don't miss this type of content or our other videos!

Songs of Thieves Statistics

It’s been almost a month since the Thieves arrived in Reverie. Did they cause the trouble we were hoping for? Let's take a look at some stats together!

(Updated statistics compared to the presentation video)

The Thieves have been deadly, with a total of 264,273 heroes killed since they began their ambush! The stats show that Elite Assassins are the main culprits behind this massacre, with over 171,928 heroes slain!

But you got your revenge! You’ve been ruthless to Stingy Jack, defeating him over 107,520 times, with an 80% success rate and an average of 30 seconds to beat him!

Since the draw is random, it’s hard to share any statistics with you on Melodies. However, we can tell you that the 3 Melodies that come up most often are:

Melody

Effect

Randomly Picked

Merry Men Melody

The locks no longer need keys to be opened; keys now grant 150 Dream Shards each

71,160 times

Sheherazad Melody

Dreams obtained from the Sandman are restocked 1 time after being purchased

69,547 times

Long John Silver Melody

Reveals all Raven Marks on the map

69,010 times

Overall (and since we launched the game in Early Access back in 2023), you've played over 300 million hours of Ravenswatch, for a total of more than 40 million runs! That's completely wild!

Monthly Challenge

Starting this month, we’re running a set of Ravenswatch challenges on our Discord server! We'll hold a themed challenge that runs for several weeks, giving everyone a chance to participate. 

This month, and to go with our recent release of the Songs of Thieves update, we have curated a special Aladdin challenge! Who better than the Master Thief to steal the thunder?

Event

End date to participate: June 30, 2026 at 4:59 PM UTC Results will be announced on: July 1, 2026

To be qualified

Mandatory

  • You must play solo and pick Aladdin

    [/*]

  • Win a run (Victory screen)

    [/*]

Apply these modifiers

  • Living the Dream (500 Shards at the start)

    [/*]

  • Solar Eclipse (Eternal Night)

    [/*]

  • Inflation (costs that include Shards are doubled)

    [/*]

About your entry

  • One entry per player

    [/*]

  • You can choose any difficulty level

    [/*]

  • Record your point of view from the Lobby until the Result Screen. Please show your stats at the end

    [/*]

Prizes

Three winners will be selected at random among the participants! That’s right, you only need to participate for a chance at winning game keys, DLCs, or Skin Packs!

For the Master Thief Challenge, each winner will receive:

  • 1x Ravenswatch - Mercenaries Skin Pack digital key (Steam)

    [/*]

  • 1x Styx: Blades of Greed digital key (Steam, PlayStation 5, or Xbox Series)

    [/*]

  • A shiny exclusive and temporary Discord role: @Master Thief

    [/*]

How to participate?

Upload the video to YouTube (public or unlisted, but not private) using the #MasterThiefChallenge in the title or the description, and share it on our official Discord server.

Head to our Discord server to share your entry; there's still time!

▶ Passtech Games' Official Discord Server ◀

What's Next?

Next Hero DLC

Our new roadmap kicked off with the Songs of Thieves update last month! We are currently hard at work on the next Hero DLC for an estimated release this fall (accompanied by a free update focused on a Talents overhaul for all Heroes).

We'll share more information as we get closer to the release date. Do you have any guesses already?

Nintendo Switch 2 Edition

Ravenswatch is coming to Nintendo Switch 2 this fall with a free Upgrade Pack for players who already own the game on Switch 1.

Ravenswatch’s Nintendo Switch 2 Edition will also be available on the eShop for players who haven’t got the game already on their Nintendo consoles. In addition to this, we’ll be releasing a limited-edition physical Switch 2 Edition of the game, including many DLCs!

We’ll share more details about what this new version will look like, but you can expect significant performance improvements and a nice visual upgrade.

Stay tuned for more information about Ravenswatch’s Nintendo Switch 2 Edition, coming out this Fall!

Community Creations

@Sergey SerSpiriT -

https://www.artstation.com/serspirit

@Vincent PEZET -

https://www.artstation.com/vincent_pzt

@Pavel Yurev

https://www.artstation.com/afteral

After a flying start to the year, the Passtech team is getting ready to enter Summer mode with a well-deserved break!

2026 is already looking bright with the shipping of the Merlin Hero DLC earlier this year, the Songs of Thieves update just last month, and a new Pégase award for the Best Game as a Service for Ravenswatch!

A summer break doesn’t mean we won’t have an eye on what the community is about, so keep sending us your feedback on the game and our latest update! We’ve also got some fun community challenges planned. Feel free to join us on Discord!

We’ll see you soon!

- The Passtech Team 🍉

Passtech Games Discord Ravenswatch Twitter Passtech Games Twitter Ravenswatch Subreddit Passtech Games Twitch Passtech Games Website

items.content:
SERVER MAINTENANCE

EDIT: Maintenance is now complete! You can resume online co-op games. Thanks for your patience :)

Greetings, Ravens!

Our servers will be offline starting at 8 am UTC for routine maintenance. During this time, you won't be able to play online co-op. Solo runs are not affected.

Maintenance shouldn't take too long! We'll let you know when it's finished.

🌎 Time zones:

  • 1 am PDT

    [/*]

  • 4 am EDT

    [/*]

  • 9 am BST

    [/*]

  • 10 am CEST

    [/*]

  • 6 pm AEST

    [/*]

  • 4 pm CST

    [/*]

Thank you for your understanding!

  • The Passtech Team 🍉

    [/*]

items.title: The Songs of Thieves Free Update is live!
items.summary: Greetings, Ravens! The Songs of Thieves update is now live on all platforms. The whole team is thrilled to share this new free update for Ravenswatch! Update your game now for new enemies, new strategies to try out, and the soft sound of Me
items.content:
Greetings, Ravens!

The Songs of Thieves update is now live on all platforms. The whole team is thrilled to share this new free update for Ravenswatch!

Update your game now for new enemies, new strategies to try out, and the soft sound of Melodies! You can read about all the new content in the patch notes link below.

The Mercenaries Skin Pack is also available on all platforms for $3,99. Wear a new costume for Geppetto, Aladdin, Snow Queen, Sun Wukong, and the Pied Piper:

Link

Watch the Songs of Thieves Launch Trailer

The launch trailer will be available on May 27 at 5:30pm GMT. Watch it live at the AG French Direct!

Summary

IT'S AN AMBUSH! THIEVES ARE COMING TO REVERIE!

  • New enemy faction: the Thieves!

    [/*]

  • Thieves' Stashes, including Treasure rooms, Trap rooms, Cursed rooms, and Mirror rooms...

    [/*]

  • Magical Harps and their swift Melodies...

    [/*]

  • Stingy Jack, a new threat to your loot!

    [/*]

  • Multiplayer Pause

    [/*]

  • And more!

    [/*]

Read the detailed patch notes here:

https://store.steampowered.com/news/app/2071280/view/661608777565341541

Get the free Update and Cosmetic DLC

Update the game for free on Steam to start playing the Songs of Thieves update.

Get Ravenswatch for 60% off on Steam:

Get the Mercenaries Skin Pack for $3,99:

Updated Roadmap

We're kicking off this new Roadmap with the Songs of Thieves free update! Our next beat will reveal a new Hero to join the Ravenswatch. Any guesses already?

Social Channels

Passtech Games Discord Ravenswatch Twitter Passtech Games Twitter Ravenswatch Subreddit Passtech Games Twitch Passtech Games Website - The Passtech Team 🍉

items.title: Ravenswatch Patch Notes (Songs of Thieves Update) - Version 1.05
items.summary: Greetings, Ravens! The Songs of Thieves update is now live on all platforms. The whole team is thrilled to share this new free update for Ravenswatch! Update your game now for new enemies, new strategies to try out, and the soft sound of Me
items.content:
Greetings, Ravens!

The Songs of Thieves update is now live on all platforms. The whole team is thrilled to share this new free update for Ravenswatch!

Update your game now for new enemies, new strategies to try out, and the soft sound of Melodies! You can read about all the new content in the patch notes below.

Watch the Songs of Thieves Launch Trailer

The launch trailer will be available on May 27 at 5:30pm GMT. Watch it live at the AG French Direct!

TL;DR

Because we know our patch notes are lengthy ones, and you probably just want to play, here’s a “too long, didn’t read!” summary just for you:

Free Update

  • New enemy faction: the Thieves!

    [/*]

  • Thieves' Stashes, including Treasure rooms, Trap rooms, Cursed rooms, and Mirror rooms…

    [/*]

  • Magical Harps and their swift Melodies…

    [/*]

  • Stingy Jack, a new threat to your loot!

    [/*]

  • Multiplayer Pause

    [/*]

  • Community QoL and Bug fixes

    [/*]

  • And more!

    [/*]

Cosmetic DLC

  • Mercenary Outfit for Aladdin

    [/*]

  • Mercenary Outfit for Sun Wukong

    [/*]

  • Mercenary Outfit for Snow Queen

    [/*]

  • Mercenary Outfit for the Pied Piper

    [/*]

  • Mercenary Outfit for Geppetto

    [/*]

Get the Mercenaries Skin Pack:

Read the detailed patch note below for more information.

Patch Notes (1.5)

🐦 = Inspired by community reports and suggestions

Due to major content changes, any existing run in progress has been discarded. Your overall progress has not been affected. Finish your current run before updating the game if you don’t want to lose it! Thank you for your understanding.

Free content and features

New content

  • New Enemy Faction - 3 types of Thieves can be found in Reverie:

    • Assassin (Elite)

      [/*]

    • Marksman (Range)

      [/*]

    • Brigand (Melee)

      [/*]

    [/*]

  • Thieves' Stashes - Located in Dark Hills, Storm Island, and Avalon, they hide secret rooms protected by Thieves:

    • Treasure room: Fill your pockets with Dream Shards!

      [/*]

    • Combat room: Avoid traps while ambushed by Thieves!

      [/*]

    • Cursed room: Challenge yourself with a random cursed ability!

      [/*]

    • 🐦 Magic Mirror room: Duplicate items thanks to the spooky face of the Magic Mirror!

      [/*]

    [/*]

  • New Mechanic - Melodies:

    • Locate Magical Harps in Thieves’ Stashes or in Reverie to collect notes

      [/*]

    • Recompose the 12 Melodies to apply powerful effects on your party, for the whole run

      • Otohime Melody: The Hourglass of Dreams empties 25% slower

        [/*]

      • Lady of the Lake Melody: Healing Fountains and Sacrificial Idols grant +100% VITALITY or DMG

        [/*]

      • 🐦Lucky Hans Melody: Magical Object collections require 1 item less to be considered completed

        [/*]

      • Galahad Melody: Grants 1 Raven's Feather and fully heals all Heroes at the start of each day or night

        [/*]

      • Hansel and Gretel Melody: When the Master Nightmare is awakened, it grants 8 DMG every 20 seconds until the final showdown begins

        [/*]

      • 🐦 Tortoise Melody: Increases move speed for all Heroes by 20%

        [/*]

      • Merry Men Melody: The locks no longer need keys to be opened; keys now grant 150 Dream Shards each

        [/*]

      • Long John Silver Melody: Reveals all Raven Marks on the map

        [/*]

      • Sheherazad Melody: Dreams obtained from the Sandman are restocked 1 time after being purchased

        [/*]

      • The Goose Girl Melody: In combat, every 15 seconds, each hero unleashes a powerful wind that blows around them, dealing 150 area damage

        [/*]

      • Fairy Godmother Melody: The group of heroes instantly levels up

        [/*]

      • The Little Tailor Melody: Grants 3 consumables each time the group of Heroes reaches a level above 10

        [/*]
      [/*]

    • Melodies can be consulted in the Magical Objects compendium

      [/*]

    • Some Melodies won’t appear if specific Modifiers are applied (to prevent the effects from becoming obsolete and anti-synergistic)

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • Stingy Jack:

    • Stingy Jack will loot chests in Reverie and run away when he sees you coming!

      [/*]

    • Hitting him will release Dream Shards and, ultimately, the Magical Object he stole.

      [/*]

    • Stingy Jack can also release a Note (related to Melody).

      [/*]

    • Don’t let him escape with your loot!

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • 🐦 Multiplayer Pause:

    • Any player can pause the game in Multiplayer

      [/*]

    • Other players can resume a game after a short time (30 sec)

      [/*]

    • You can navigate Options, the Map, and your Inventory while the game is paused

      [/*]

    • Multiplayer pauses are unlimited in a code or friends’ invite party

      [/*]

    • Multiplayer pauses are limited to 3 per player in a matchmaking party to prevent abuse

      [/*]
    [/*]

General Adjustments

  • Map Generation:

    • 🐦 Tumors are no longer part of the mandatory generation

      [/*]

    • 🐦 (Red) Boss Dens will no longer spawn in Green zones on Dark Hills

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • Refugees:

    • 🐦 Increase in rewards for most refugees:

      • Warm up the Child: Gain 100 Shards (from 60)

        [/*]

      • Pay for Healing?: Heal 100% of life and gain +15 VITALITY (from 10)

        [/*]

      • Sacrifice Health for power?: Gain +10 Attack Points (from 4)

        [/*]

      • Sacrifice Health for power?: Gain +10 Armor Points (from 6)

        [/*]

      • Accept Blessing?: Increase Critical Chances by 10% and increase Critical Damage by 15% (from 4% and 13%)

        [/*]

      • Accept the curse of the Mad Man?: Get Cursed Special status for 90 seconds + 15 Attack Points (from 10 Attack Points)

        [/*]

      • Accept the curse of the Mad Man?: Get Cursed Power status for 90 seconds + 15 Attack Points (from 10 Attack Points)

        [/*]

      • Accept the curse of the Mad Man?: Get Cursed Defense status for 90 seconds + 15 Attack Points (from 10 Attack Points)

        [/*]

      • Accept the Artefact?: Gain +20 VITALITY (from 5)

        [/*]

      • Give Blood?: Increase by 10% Crit chances (from 6%)

        [/*]

      • Give Blood?: Increase by 20% Crit chances (from 18%)

        [/*]

      • Choose an object to give up (Epic): Gain 450 Shards (from 350)

        [/*]

      • Choose an object to give up (Rare): Gain 300 Shards (from 210)

        [/*]

      • Donate Attack or Armor?: Gain 200 Shards (from 175)

        [/*]

      • Donate Star Rolls?: Gain 160 Shards (from 100)

        [/*]

      • Donate Star Rolls?: Gain 3 Power Ups (from 2)

        [/*]

      • Accept Gift?: Gain 100 Shards (from 50)

        [/*]

      • Use the Cursed Knowledge?: Gain 15 Attack Points and Gain Cursed Status for 90 seconds (from 10 Attack Points)

        [/*]

      • Use the Cursed Knowledge?: Gain 40 SHIELD (from 20)

        [/*]

      • Drink the Potion?: Gain 50 SHIELD (from 25)

        [/*]

      • Drink the Potion?: Gain STRENGH status for 90 seconds (from 60sec)

        [/*]

      • Pay for Information?: Reveal 3 locations on the map (from 1)

        [/*]

      • Pay the Crafter?: Pay 100 Shards (from 150)

        [/*]
      [/*]
    [/*]

Heroes improvements, fixes, and balancing

  • Merlin:

    • 🐦 HUD: Added Runes Icons on the HUD to keep track of the number of runes active (located above Merlin’s Trait icon)

      [/*]

    • 🐦Starfall (Ability): Increased damage of each star to 70/105/126/140 (from 60/90/108/120)

      [/*]

    • 🐦 Trinity (Talent): Collecting the object now grants 2 Stars of Fate (from 1)

      [/*]

    • 🐦 Grace of Heaven (Talent): Trait cooldown reduction is now set at 2/2.5/3/3.5sec (from 4/5/6/7sec)

      [/*]

    • Brambles Whirlwind (Talent): Fixed damage bonus so it is now correctly additive with other Power bonuses (was multiplicative before)

      [/*]

    • 🐦 Life Seed (Talent): 

      • Healing range has been increased to 12m (from 10m)

        [/*]

      • Duration has been increased to 2sec (from 1.5sec)

        [/*]
      [/*]

    • 🐦 Retaliation (Talent): 

      • Increased Shield explosion damage to 80/100/120/140 (from 60/75/90/105)

        [/*]

      • Stagger damage has been increased to 30 (from 5)

        [/*]

      • The explosion radius has been increased to 6m (from 5m)

        [/*]
      [/*]
    [/*]

Design Notes: We’re preparing major adjustments and Talent replacements for all Heroes in our next update, but we still wanted to address some of the fresh feedback received from Merlin’s Hero DLC in the Songs of Thieves update.

Quality of Life improvements

  • 🐦 Chests and Drops: Rare/Epic Magical Objects are now guaranteed after rerolling an Orange/Red chest or a dropped object

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Charge Cap: Increased the cap for the number of charges of an Ability from 9 to 20

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Oni Mask (Magical Object): Oni Mask values now display current DMG earned and ARMOR lost

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Moonstone (Magical Object): Moonstone values now display current DMG earned

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Dragon Hide (Magical Object): Dragon Hide values now display current ARMOR earned

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Sandman’s Shop: Current values are now displayed on Magical Objects sold by the Sandman

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Results Screen: More than 20 Magical Objects can now be displayed in the end-of-run statistics

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Localization:

    • Magical Objects: Fixed the inconsistencies in the capitalisation of Magical Objects' names

      [/*]

    • Flash of Genius (Geppetto): Clarify text to specify that the effect applies only once per run

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • 🐦 General: Fixed several typos, mistranslations, broken variables in Italian, Spanish, Korean, German, Russian and Simplified Chinese

    [/*]

Fixed bugs

General

  • 🐦 Savior of the Day (Achievement): Fixed an issue where Merlin's Blood Ritual Ultimate would not unlock "Savior of the Day" achievement

    [/*]

  • Legacy (Modifier): Fixed an issue where it was possible to have multiple Legendary Magical Objects drops

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Astral Shrine relearning talents: Fixing an issue where the chances of having better rarity talents were incorrect. Chances are much higher now

    [/*]

  • 🐦 Results screen: Fixed the icons missing when continuing a saved run in the Results screen

    [/*]

Heroes

  • Scarlet:

    • In the Belly (Ultimate): Fixed an issue where the MARKED status was not transferred after using ‘In the Belly’ Ultimate

      [/*]

    • 🐦 Transformation: Scarlet can no longer transform while interacting with menus (to avoid being abruptly kicked out of a menu)

      [/*]

    [/*]

  • The Pied Piper:

    • Star Rain (Talent): Fixed an issue where the Talent ‘Star Rain’ effect was not cancelled when the Ultimate ‘Unchained Melody’ was cancelled itself

      [/*]

    [/*]

  • Beowulf:

    • Dragon Flight (Ultimate): Fixed an issue where Dragon Flight’s tooltip damage value was not impacted by the Talent ‘Sudden Growth’

      [/*]

    [/*]

  • Sun Wukong:

    • 🐦 Fiery Golden Eyes (Talent): Fix an issue where the Talent would grant +120% extra shards instead of 20%

      [/*]

    • 🐦 Transfiguration (Ultimate): Fixed an issue where stats earned from sources other than Magical Objects were removed after Sun Wukong’s ‘Transfiguration’

      [/*]

    [/*]

items.content:
Publisher Nacon and developer Passtech Games announced the roguelike action game, Ravenswatch , will launch for the Nintendo Switch 2 this fall.

The game is currently available for the PlayStation 5 , Xbox Series X|S, Nintendo Switch , PlayStation 4 , Xbox One , and PC via Steam .

The free "Song of Thieves" update will release on May 27, which adds the following the content:

View the Song of Thieves update trailer below:

items.title: Ravenswatch Getting Switch 2 Version, Free Update
Ravenswatch coming to Nintendo Switch 2
items.summary: Nacon and Passtech Games are making a native Nintendo Switch 2 version of Ravenswatch, they’ve announced. It will be available this fall. Specifics about what’s planned for the Nintendo Switch 2 version have yet to be provided. Compared to the Nintendo Switch release though, expect to see technical improvements. Also announced today is that Ravenswatch is getting a new free...
items.content:
Nacon and Passtech Games are making a native Nintendo Switch 2 version of Ravenswatch, they’ve announced. It will be available this fall.

Specifics about what’s planned for the Nintendo Switch 2 version have yet to be provided. Compared to the Nintendo Switch release though, expect to see technical improvements.

Also announced today is that Ravenswatch is getting a new free update, “Song of Thieves” on May 27, 2026. It includes new enemies and activities plus innovative ways to strengthen your heroes.

Ravenswatch is a top-down roguelike action game in which you play as heroes of tales and legends that must slay hordes of nightmarish creatures in nerve-racking fights. After the success of Curse of the Dead Gods©, Passtech Games returns with an improved formula and an addictive game experience with endless replay value. It can be played solo or with up to four players in co-op.

Play as 9 heroes, each with a unique twist and individual gameplay, including Little Red Riding Hood, who suffers from lycanthropy; Sun Wukong, the fallen monkey king; the Snow Queen with her evil powers; and many other familiar characters who have a darker side and are more violent than you might remember.

The Nightmare has invaded the world of dreams and is corrupting everything in its wake. Faced with this evil, the Ravenswatch is gathering heroes from tales and legends from all over the world to lead a crucial battle and restore the fragile balance. But the heroes themselves have been corrupted. How deep do the roots of this evil go?

To defeat the Nightmare and reach the final chapter, you will need a strong build, focus and/or excellent teamwork. You have 3 days and 3 nights to increase your power. Face the chapter boss when you feel ready… or when you no longer have a choice.

During your game, optimize your time and make the best use of the upgrades you earn. With each new run, explore a new map filled with activities and enemies. Unlock new heroes and new content from one run to the next.

A trailer showing off the new update can be found below.

Get more news updates about Nintendo Switch 2 here .

items.title: Ravenswatch: Songs of Thieves - Official Free Update Teaser Trailer | Nacon Connect 2026
items.summary: Check out the new Ravenswatch trailer to see what you can expect with the free Songs of Thieves update, which brings new enemies, new challenges, and new rewards.

items.title: The Songs of Thieves Update (Content Highlights)
items.summary: Greetings, everyone! It's time to reveal a little more about Ravenswatch's upcoming free update, “Songs of Thieves” ! New enemies, new rooms to explore, brand-new gameplay mechanics... In this post, we’ll explain it all! (Or almost everythi
items.content:
Greetings, everyone!

It's time to reveal a little more about Ravenswatch's upcoming free update, “Songs of Thieves”!

New enemies, new rooms to explore, brand-new gameplay mechanics... In this post, we’ll explain it all! (Or almost everything...) Spoiler alert: read at your own risk if you want to keep the surprise.

Join us on May 7 for Nacon Connect, where we’ll reveal exclusive gameplay footage from the "Songs of Thieves" update, along with its release date.

Thieves’ Stashes

In this video, environment artists Arthur and Angelica from Passtech Games reveal all the secrets behind the creation of the Thieves’ Stashes!

But what are Thieves' Shashes?

Across Dark Hills, Storm Island, and Avalon, Thieves have set up camp in Reverie and are hiding their precious loot there. You’ll discover 4 unique types of rooms deep within their hideouts, each with its own challenges or rewards. 

Combat Rooms

Combat rooms are filled with traps, a new mechanic borrowed from our previous game: Curse of the Dead Gods! Watch out for explosive poison barrels, cannonballs, and flaming runes in these secret rooms.

Treasure Rooms

Free Shards, literally! Fill your pockets in the Thieves' Treasure rooms. So this is what a dream come true looks like?

Cursed Rooms

Cursed rooms will challenge your skill when being amputated from one of your abilities during the whole fight. Hopefully, it won’t curse your favorite one…?

Mirror Rooms

Make the Magic Mirror happy by asking it to duplicate your Magical Objects! This new feature, which the community has been eagerly awaiting, is sure to delight item collectors…

New Enemy Faction

Featuring three different enemies, this new faction will make its debut in the world of Reverie in the upcoming Ravenswatch update! Make way for Bandits, Marksmen, and Assassins. 

Each with their own specialty (long-range attacks, quick strikes, etc.), the Thieves are highly agile and possess exceptional reflexes.

In addition to being fierce foes, Thieves will also pose a threat to your Dream Shards’ purses. Don’t get hit if you don’t want to end up with empty pockets. Nothing to worry about, right?

Melodies

With the “Songs of Thieves” update, as you wander through Reverie or explore the Thieves' Stashes, you'll come across mysterious Harps along the way. Activating this magical instrument will release enchanted musical notes.

By composing melodies using musical notes, you'll unlock powerful effects that affect your entire run. With some examples, you'll understand better:

Long John Silver Melody

Reveals all Raven's landing marks on the map.

Tortoise Melody

Increases the move speed of all Heroes by 20%.

Merry Men Melody

Locks no longer need keys to be opened; they now grant 150 Dream Shards each.

It’s up to you to rearrange the 12 available melodies and incorporate them into your strategies!

Stingy Jack

Thieves aren't the only swindlers showing up in Reverie. Every now and then, you might run into the infamous Stingy Jack and his turnip head.

This sneaky character will be one step ahead, stripping the chests of their treasures.

It’s up to you to reclaim what’s yours before he escapes! While you’re at it, you can also pad your pockets with his own riches…

Multiplayer Pause & QoL

We’ve also included a number of quality-of-life improvements in this update, such as the highly requested Multiplayer Pause feature!

You’ll now be able to pause during co-op matches. Any player can pause the game in Multiplayer, and other party members can resume it after a short time, preventing getting stuck indefinitely in a run. While in pause, players can navigate to the Options, the Map, and their Inventory.

We'll share more information about the Multiplayer Pause and other QoL improvements in the patch notes.

Mercenaries Skin Pack

A new Mercenaries-themed Skin Pack will be released alongside the "Songs of Thieves" update! Heroes will be featured with special premium Outfits enhanced with 3D model alterations and VFX.

Snow Queen – The Snow Queen goes rogue in this new wintery Outfit, complemented by ice daggers duly forged to be thrown at the worst Nightmares of Reverie.

The Pied Piper – Who called the Plague Doctor? Become the Black Death in this Outfit dripping with green slime. Even your rats are joining you in this new role!

Aladdin – What if the best thieves were pirates? Wearing a bandana and wielding scimitars that have been turned into sabers, Aladdin gives off vibes straight from the Caribbean.

Sun Wukong – Sun Wukong transforms into a shadowy red ninja by donning a hood and wielding, as always, with flawless precision, this magnificent new staff.

Geppetto – Would you trust this Geppetto, the traveling merchant? In this Outfit, Geppettot traded his trunk for a backpack filled with curiosities and a Hammer-Lantern.

The Mercenaries Skin Pack will be available on all platforms for $3,99.

Nacon Connect!

Bookmark the next Nacon Connect event for an exclusive look at the Songs of Thieves update! The event will also be filled with new announcements from our publisher Nacon, so don’t miss it!

Nacon Connect:

📆 May 7

🕚 8 PM CEST

🔗 Watch Live on YouTube

We cannot wait.

  • The Passtech Team 🍉

    [/*]

items.title: Ravenswatch reveals new content roadmap outlining updates through 2027
items.summary: Starting with the Song of Thieves update this spring, the roadmap includes several paid and free content drops.
items.content:
It looks like Ravenswatch fans are eating well, as developer Nacon has revealed a content roadmap outlining updates planned through 2027.

As detailed below, this latest wave of content kicks off with the Song of Thieves update this spring. It promises to introduce thieves as a new enemy faction, alongside new room types and a new NPC known as Stingy Jack. A paid cosmetic DLC will also be released around the same time.

The next portion of the roadmap isn’t as detailed, but it confirms that two new DLC heroes, new game modes, and a rework of hero talents are on the way. Encouragingly, the team at Nacon plans to deliver both paid and free updates, ensuring all players get something out of future content.

On the roadmap, developer Passtech said that both it and Nancon “are incredibly proud to have completed one full year of post-launch support for Ravenswatch.”

If you’ve yet to check out Ravenswatch, the game is currently on sale at 50% off on Steam (£10.49) until March 11th.

Mike reviewed the game back when it was exclusively available for PC. In his review, he said: “All told, Ravenswatch is a very good action-roguelite with a lot of subtle depth and nuance just waiting to be discovered.”

You can check out the trailer for the new roadmap below:

Still with me? Of course you are! If you want to keep reading about great hand-picked roguelikes, this website is absolutely chock-full of them. Head over to the Guides Page for a concentrated hit of roguish games, organised via sub-genre and platform.

What’s more, there are always going to be new lists for emerging trends as well as fresh titles to highlight. This is because my backlog is embarrassingly long, and so I’m always trying to balance the new games that arrive in my inbox with playing the stuff I already have.

Finally, if you would like to keep track of our original reporting and support the work that we do here in this most niche corner of the Interweb, please make sure we’re one of your trusted sources on Google News . Alternatively, there’s an RSS feed , if you prefer.

You can also join our communities on Reddit and Bluesky , where I/we post all of our content and where you can share your thoughts and recommendations. We also have a burgeoning presence on YouTube . Come and say hello; we try to keep all of these spaces welcoming!

Finally, if you’d like to support Rogueliker more directly, you can always leave us a tip over on our BuyMeACoffee page. Thank you!

items.title: Passtech Games lays out what to expect from Ravenswatch's next two years
items.content:
0 Copied! Copied! Comment now! Subscribe to our newsletter here! * Required field E-mail * Name * --> HQ initPlayerScript('780063'); Developer Passtech Games has been steadily improving and expanding Ravenswatch ever since the game initially launched as an Early Access project. To this end, support is not slowing down anytime soon and now we know about the next two years of content plans for the game.

The 2026 and 2027 roadmap has been shared to reveal what the game will be offering up over the next 22 months. There is plenty of content on the table, but in a rather unusual manner, we don't really know when the content will actually debut in the game within this time frame.

The only confirmed window is for the Songs of Thieves update that will launch this spring as a free addition to the game, which will bring a new enemy faction (the Thieves), a new room type, Melodies, a new encounter in Reverie with Stingy Jack, and the multiplayer pause function too.

After this, expect two new DLC heroes to arrive, new game modes, hero talent reworks, DLC skin packs, quality-of-life and balance changes, and more. Again, there is no timeline on when any of this will arrive other than before 2028, but you can see the full roadmap for a teaser of what's to come below.

Related texts 0 Ravenswatch REVIEW. Written by Marco Vrolijk on the 9th of December 2024 at 10 A dark and atmospheric Roguelike set back by a lack of storyline.

items.title: What's next for Ravenswatch? (Roadmap Reveal)
items.summary: Greetings, Ravens! We're here! The teams at Passtech Games and NACON are incredibly proud to have completed one full year of post-launch support for Ravenswatch. All the way from January 2025 with the Nightmares Unleashed update, up until r
items.content:
Greetings, Ravens!

We're here! The teams at Passtech Games and NACON are incredibly proud to have completed one full year of post-launch support for Ravenswatch.

All the way from January 2025 with the Nightmares Unleashed update, up until recently with the release of Merlin's Hero DLC, we have been hard at work expanding the world of Ravenswatch and its defenders.

Is it... The End?

We've prepared a Year 1 retrospective of what Ravenswatch went through during this epic time. And... maybe there's a little suprise at the end of the video?

After all these updates, the Ravenswatch community has shown us how much they care about the game! We couldn't be more grateful for your support, thank you!

Let's continue with another round of updates and DLCs in 2026 and 2027!

2026-2027 Roadmap

Here we go again! We're happy to reveal our 2026-2027 plans for Ravenswatch. We'll reveal more details about future updates as we get closer to their release. Thanks for your support!

We still have a few ideas in the bag for more content and new Heroes. Can you guess who may join the Ravenswatch? 👀

This roadmap presents 2026 and 2027 plans for Ravenswatch as of March 2nd, 2026, and may be subject to changes before implementation.

Upcoming: Songs of Thieves Update

The Songs of Thieves update is a Free Major Update planned for release this Spring.

What could the Thieves be hiding in their treasure rooms and deadly traps? Maybe completing the various melodies will make you stronger, but watch out for shard theft!

This new update will add:

  • A new enemy faction: the Thieves!

    [/*]

  • New room types, including a Magic Mirror?!

    [/*]

  • Melodies and their powerful effect...

    [/*]

  • A strange encounter in Reverie: Stingy Jack

    [/*]

  • The long-awaited Multiplayer Pause

    [/*]

Additionally, we will release the Mercenaries Skin Pack (Paid DLC) for the most devious players on the battlefield.

Catch up with Year One with this Bundle at a discounted price:

- The Passtech Team 🍉

items.title: Ravenswatch Roadmap for 2026 and 2027 Promises New DLC and More
items.summary: Ravenswatch is still very much alive and kicking, and its developers have just released a new roadmap that promises plenty of things to look forward to over the next year or so.
items.content:
Developer Passtech Games and publisher Nacon have released a brand new Ravenswatch roadmap, and it's chock-full of stuff coming to the action roguelike over the next year or so.

In a Steam post , the two studios first express pride in completing "one full year of post-launch support" for the game before promising another year of updates and DLC to look forward to.

At the moment, the roadmap is a little barebones; Passtech and Nacon say they will reveal more about each upcoming update and DLC pack as release draws nearer, so for now, you'll have to content yourself with a brief overview of what's in store for Ravenswatch .

There's plenty to look forward to in Ravenswatch this year. The first major update that's coming to Ravenswatch in 2026 is Songs of Thieves, which will, as the name suggests, bring the Thieves faction to the game. You can also look forward to new room types, a new NPC, and a paid skin pack if you're the type who likes to admire your heroes.

Following that, a new hero will be introduced as part of a second paid DLC pack, with a talent rework also being included in a minor free update. All of this will be followed by updates introducing new game modes and quality-of-life changes, as well as more paid DLC.

News of Ravenswatch 's second year of content comes after publisher Nacon delayed its upcoming Nacon Connect presentation to May, which itself follows the publisher filing for insolvency just days prior .

Nacon's desire to continue supporting Ravenswatch makes a lot of sense in this environment, too; the game is the publisher's second most popular on Steam by player count (at time of writing).

You can check out Ravenswatch right now on PC , PlayStation , Xbox , and Nintendo Switch (although there's still no native Switch 2 version, unfortunately). Stay tuned for more on this and all other things Nacon.

items.title: Ravenswatch Community Spotlight (February 2026)
items.summary: Greetings, Ravens! A new Community Spotlight is here! With Merlin's release last month, we've got a lot to talk about. Let's jump in. On today's Community Spotlight: New episode of News from Reverie [/*] Merlin's stats after 1 month [/*] Ro
items.content:
Greetings, Ravens!

A new Community Spotlight is here! With Merlin's release last month, we've got a lot to talk about. Let's jump in.

On today's Community Spotlight:

  • New episode of News from Reverie

    [/*]

  • Merlin's stats after 1 month

    [/*]

  • Romeo & Juliet Discord event

    [/*]

  • A new Pégase nomination!

    [/*]

  • Sneak peek at our next update!

    [/*]

News from Reverie (February 2026)

Watch our latest News from Reverie episode to gather all the information on this month's Community Spotlight. Subscribe to our YouTube channel for the latest Ravenswatch and Passtech Games news!

Merlin's Stats

The wizard arrived on the battlefield and left no one indifferent! On behalf of everyone on the team, thank you for your incredible support! Let's take a look at this cool infographic.

561 000 runs were played with him, but we also counted 1,4 million deaths. Aoutch!

What were your favorite Talents?

  • Runic Might was your go to Starter with a 42% pick rate.

    [/*]

  • For regular upgrades, Celestral Wrath was number one on the podium with a 73% pick rate.

    [/*]

  • Sacred Strike, second with 70% pick rate.

    [/*]

  • Followed by Brambles Whirlwind with a 69% pick rate.

    [/*]

Your favorite Ultimate? You probably already know it, it’s Forbidden Spells with a 74% pick rate!

Also, a patch was released this week to address some of the bugs reported on version 1.4:

Romeo & Juliet Discord Event

To celebrate Valentine’s Day and our precious Romeo & Juliet, we’ve organized a romantic challenge over on our Discord server. The goal is simple: score the most points to become the best couple in Rêverie!

Get bonus points to increase your score:

  • Wear the Signature Outfits (Groom and Bride) = 25 points

    [/*]

  • Per Talent linked to TRAIT (at the end of the run) = 25 points

    [/*]

  • Choose Kiss the Bride and Kiss the Groom as Starting Talents = 50 points

    [/*]

  • Per use of Wild Waltz (Ultimate) = 50 points

    [/*]

  • Complete the Quest = 100 points

    [/*]

  • Equip a matching band (2x Ring of Dispel or 2x Nibelungen Ring) = 100 points

    [/*]

  • Unlock something with a Key = 100 points

    [/*]

  • Use NO Health fountain = 150 points

    [/*]

  • NO Deathdoors = 150 points

    [/*]

  • Complete a Magical Object collection = 150 points (one time)

    [/*]

  • Kill the Master Nightmare with a SPECIAL (Passionate Kiss) = 200 points

    [/*]

  • Create a “Kiss Cam” edit (Passionate Kiss best-of) = 200 points

    [/*]

  • Play with your actual partner = 0 points (but GG)

    [/*]

There’s still time to enter the competition! Head to our Discord server and upload your entries to YouTube to participate 💘

The challenge ends on Sunday, February 22nd at 11:59 CET

Participate in Passtech's Discord

Pégase Nomination

Here we go again! Ravenswatch scored a nomination at the 2026 Pégases. The award ceremony, organized by the Académie des Arts et Techniques du Jeu Vidéo, will recognize French developers and their games on March 5th.

We are honored to appear in the Best Game as a Service category alongside Asobo’s Flight Simulator 2024 and Ubisoft’s Assassin’s Creed Mirage. This category celebrates games that had an existence beyond their launch, and we’re proud to be represented here with Ravenswatch.

As you know, our first year of post-launch support for Ravenswatch has been a tremendous success. Winning this award would be a great recognition of our work since version 1.0.

If you’re a French academician, go vote for us! We’ll see on March 5th!

Next update sneak peek

We’re this 🤏 close to unveiling our plans for Ravenswatch’s Year 2 of post-launch support.

This new roadmap will showcase our ideas for 2026 and 2027. We’re not ready to share the full details yet, but we didn’t want to leave you empty-handed! 

Lo and behold…. The Thieves! 

Thieves will invade the world of Reverie in our next major free update! You might recognize their faces from an earlier roadmap, but ultimately, they didn’t make the cut in the first year of post-launch support.

They’re now back more sly than ever, and we’re cooking some interesting adjacent mechanics around them. Can’t wait to share more.

Thanks for reading this Community Spotlight. Our next post will reveal our next Ravenswatch Roadmap, stay tuned for that!

We’ll see you soon!

- The Passtech Team 🍉

Passtech Games Discord Ravenswatch Twitter Passtech Games Twitter Ravenswatch Subreddit Passtech Games Twitch Passtech Games Website
